title: cynanchica neilreichii
intro: Cynanchica neilreichii, also known as the Frasera or Mountain Goldenseal, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Central America and the southwestern United States. The medicinal properties of Cynanchica neilreichii are primarily attributed to its roots, which contain a range of bioactive compounds, including glycosides, alkaloids, and phenolic acids. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The roots can be prepared into teas, tinctures, or infusions for herbal use, and are also used in traditional medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
